document.source_title: Efforts to eliminate hepatitis delivers gains but more action needed to meet 2030 targets

claims.claim_text: Global efforts to combat viral hepatitis are delivering measurable progress.
claims.evidence_text: Global efforts to combat viral hepatitis are delivering measurable progress in reducing infections and deaths.

claims.claim_text: 1.34 million lives were claimed by hepatitis B and C in 2024.
claims.evidence_text: Viral hepatitis B and C – the two infections responsible for 95% of hepatitis-related deaths worldwide – claimed 1.34 million lives in 2024.

claims.claim_text: The annual number of new hepatitis B infections has dropped by 32%.
claims.evidence_text: The annual number of new hepatitis B infections has dropped by 32% and hepatitis C-related deaths have fallen by 12% globally.

claims.claim_text: 287 million people were living with chronic hepatitis B or C infection in 2024.
claims.evidence_text: Updated WHO estimates indicate that 287 million people were living with chronic hepatitis B or C infection in 2024.

entities.name: World Health Organization
entities.description: The organization responsible for global health issues.
entities.evidence_text: according to a new World Health Organization (WHO) report released today at the World Hepatitis Summit.

entities.name: Hepatitis B
entities.description: A viral infection that attacks the liver and can cause chronic disease.
entities.evidence_text: Viral hepatitis B and C – the two infections responsible for 95% of hepatitis-related deaths worldwide.

entities.name: Hepatitis C
entities.description: A viral infection that causes liver inflammation, sometimes leading to serious liver damage.
entities.evidence_text: Viral hepatitis B and C – the two infections responsible for 95% of hepatitis-related deaths worldwide.

events.evidence_text: Viral hepatitis B and C – the two infections responsible for 95% of hepatitis-related deaths worldwide – claimed 1.34 million lives in 2024.
The WHO African Region accounted for 68% of new hepatitis B infections.

metrics.name: New hepatitis B infections
metrics.evidence_text: That year, 0.9 million people were newly infected with hepatitis B.

metrics.name: Chronic hepatitis B treatment coverage
metrics.evidence_text: Of the 240 million people with chronic hepatitis B in 2024, fewer than 5% were receiving treatment.

citations.title: Efforts to eliminate hepatitis delivers gains but more action needed to meet 2030 targets

source_credibility.reason: The source is a report from the World Health Organization.
